home.title: Chiaki Inari Shrine
home.description: An independent circle exploring technology, type design, and subculture. Run by Chiaki Suzukaze, it also creates illustration and open-source software.

profile.title: About Chiaki - Chiaki Inari Shrine
profile.description: Hello, I’m Chiaki Suzukaze: an illustrator, designer, and programmer. I also work across open-source type, independent creative work, and fan culture.

story.title: Story - Chiaki Inari Shrine
story.description: A story where Chiaki Inari Shrine, Tokoyo, and human memory intersect—an original work set in a postwar world.

character.title: Characters - Chiaki Inari Shrine
character.description: Head of Chiaki Inari Shrine’s First Laboratory and the ▓▓th shrine maiden: Chiaki Suzukaze. Meet this original fox-eared character and her setting.

art.title: Chiaki Suzukaze’s Art - Chiaki Inari Shrine
art.description: A gallery of Chiaki Suzukaze’s character illustrations and creative work, including original concept art, outfits, and visual projects.

works.title: Works - Chiaki Inari Shrine
works.description: Software, tools, and interfaces designed and developed by Chiaki, including open-source tools, font editors, and React components.

chiakey.title: ChiaKey - Chiaki Inari Shrine
chiakey.description: A modern Traditional Chinese Zhuyin input method for macOS, based on Yahoo! KeyKey’s open-source code. It supports Apple Silicon and Intel Macs while preserving KeyKey’s familiar feel.

kumiko.title: Kumiko Font Editor - Chiaki Inari Shrine
kumiko.description: A font editor and glyph-completion tool that runs entirely in the browser. Kumiko loads UFOs from GitHub, builds glyphs with IDS components, and submits pull requests.

tgJpg.title: tg.jpg Telegram Bot - Chiaki Inari Shrine
tgJpg.description: A Telegram bot that replies with the first usable image-search result for .jpg. It also supports .png and .gif, returning the first image Telegram can send.

splitFlap.title: react-split-flap - Chiaki Inari Shrine
splitFlap.description: A React component that recreates station split-flap displays. It also animates large boards of flaps smoothly for station and airport-style displays.

tokyonoSora.title: Tokyono Sora - Chiaki Inari Shrine
tokyonoSora.description: A CSS theme that redesigns the Plurk timeline. Tokyono Sora is free and open source, with glass panels and responsive reading.

fonts.title: Fonts - Chiaki Inari Shrine
fonts.description: Typefaces designed or co-created by Chiaki, including Taiwan Railways lettering, AkiNixie numerals, and jf open Huninn.

blog.title: Blog - Chiaki Inari Shrine
blog.description: Notes and journal entries by Chiaki.

akitra.title: Taiwan Railways Typeface - Chiaki Inari Shrine
akitra.description: A typeface based on lettering found on Taiwan Railways rolling stock, with type marks, numerals, CJK characters, and 284 frequently used glyphs.

nixie.title: AkiNixie - Chiaki Inari Shrine
nixie.description: A numeral typeface that recreates the warm orange glow of Nixie tubes. AkiNixie is suited to clocks, instruments, and retro-futurist interfaces.

huninn.title: jf open Huninn - Chiaki Inari Shrine
huninn.description: A Taiwanese open-source rounded typeface by justfont, refined for Taiwanese forms and everyday reading. Chiaki contributed while at justfont.

home.title: Chiaki Inari Shrine
home.lead: An independent creative circle where illustration, type design, open-source software, and subculture research share the same worktable.
home.body: Run by Chiaki Suzukaze, Chiaki Inari Shrine turns favorite cultures into things to use, read, and share—from original illustration and merchandise to macOS input methods, web tools, and open-source typefaces.

worksPage.title: Works
worksPage.intro: From Traditional Chinese input methods and browser font tools to React components and interface themes, these projects bring familiar tools to new systems and make creative work easier to return to.

chiakey.title: ChiaKey
chiakey.description: A macOS Zhuyin input method based on the open-source Yahoo! KeyKey codebase, preserving its familiar composition feel and candidate window while maintaining it for modern systems. Supports Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.

kumiko.description: A zero-install web font editor: load a UFO from GitHub, complete glyphs with IDS components in the browser, then submit a pull request—making open-source CJK font work easier to join.

tokyono.title: Tokyono Sora
tokyono.description: A free Plurk CSS theme with glass panels, Nixie-tube karma, and responsive layout—apply it with one block of CSS. It is open source and designed for a clearer timeline.

tgJpg.description: Type an image filename in Telegram and the bot searches several sources in priority order, returning the first image it can send. Supports .jpg, .png, and .gif.

splitFlap.description: A React component that recreates station split-flap displays. A shared animation clock and the Web Animations API keep hundreds of flaps moving smoothly together, including airport- and station-style boards.

fontsPage.title: Fonts
fontsPage.intro: From lettering found on Taiwan Railways, to Nixie-tube numerals and an open-source rounded typeface refined for Taiwan, every typeface begins with a particular culture and experience.

akitra.title: Taiwan Railways Typeface
akitra.description: Based on lettering found on Taiwan Railways rolling stock, preserving visual memories of car numbers, weights, and type marks.

nixie.title: Nixie Numeral Typeface
nixie.description: Warm Nixie-display numerals for clocks and retro-futurist interfaces.

huninn.title: jf open Huninn
huninn.description: A Taiwanese open-source rounded typeface, co-created at justfont and soft and springy like tapioca pearls.

characterPage.name: Chiaki Suzukaze
characterPage.description:
Head of Chiaki Inari Shrine’s First Laboratory and the ▓▓th shrine maiden: a brown-haired fox lady with animal ears, a divine messenger.
She is kind, loves talking with people, and hopes every day can be a happy one.
Though born after the war, she loves collecting things left behind by prewar humanity, making little things by hand, and anything cute.
characterPage.r18Description: This content contains adult material and is restricted to viewers aged 18 or over.
characterPage.live2dDescription: I tried making a Live2D model—move the mouse and its eyes will follow!

header.tagline: Exploring reason through computation, painting grace one stroke at a time
header.introLine2: I draw, design, and write code—always starting new things and gradually bringing them to life.

design.title: Design
design.description: I like human-centered design that balances function and beauty. I contributed to justfont’s Huninn typeface and have also worked on graphic and web design.

drawing.title: Drawing
drawing.description: I love drawing and occasional doodling—especially maid outfits, glasses, sailor uniforms, animal ears, bunny girls, and competitive swimsuits!

photo.title: Photography
photo.description: I like using a camera to preserve things that only exist in a particular instant. Lately, I mainly shoot film.

model.title: Models
model.description: I’m fascinated by the power and beauty condensed into tiny scales. I even have an airbrush at home for painting!

doujin.title: Doujin events
doujin.description: I regularly attend FF, CWT, Comiket, and the like, and I set up a booth when I can. I love joining these events and chatting with everyone.

cosplay.title: Cosplay
cosplay.description: I love coordinating cosplays, though I don’t handle heat well, so I usually cosplay at winter events. Let’s plan a matching costume!

lolita.title: Lolita fashion
lolita.description: I love cute little dresses, especially Classic and Military Lolita, and also Amavel’s academic style and jirai-kei outfits!

travel.title: Travel
travel.description: I enjoy researching routes, exploring new places, and experiencing different cultures—especially night views and local food.

opensource.title: Open source
opensource.description: I love writing code, and I also love sharing what I’ve learned and connecting with people. You’ll often spot me at open-source conferences such as COSCUP!

electronics.title: Electronics
electronics.description: I love drawing circuits and soldering all kinds of boards. I’ve built things like amplifiers and a Nixie-tube clock.

food.title: Good food
food.description: I love taking Mr. Walrus out to try cuisines from around the world in search of amazing flavors. I also enjoy cooking and mixing cocktails myself.

mini.title: Austin Mini
mini.description: I own a 1989 classic Austin Mini and love the raw, mechanical feel of the old Mini. Curious folks are always welcome to chat!

nowPlaying.title: What I’m into lately
nowPlaying.item1: Anime and video games, especially science-fiction works such as cyberpunk and Endfield.
nowPlaying.item2: Retro things—particularly the design sensibility around the 20th century. I’ve recently started collecting vinyl records.

gettingAlong.title: Keeping in touch
gettingAlong.line1: I have a lot on my plate, so I can be slow to reply.
gettingAlong.line2: But “slow” never means “I don’t want to talk.” You’re always welcome to chat or send a message; I’ll read it and reply as soon as I can.
gettingAlong.line3: I may not see every post, especially when I’m busy. It’s never intentional, so thank you for understanding.

oneRequest.title: A small request
oneRequest.line1: I don’t talk about politics much, and I don’t enjoy harshly criticizing things.
oneRequest.line2: I don’t think there are many absolute rights or wrongs; often, things have simply been put in the wrong place.
oneRequest.line3: With that kindness and respect, I think we’ll get along well!

contact.title: Contact
contact.textBefore: My social profiles and contact details are collected

nixiePage.storyP1: Nixie tubes are numerical display components from the Cold War era: metal cathodes for 0–9 are stacked inside a glass tube, and neon gas glows warmly around a digit when powered.
nixiePage.storyP2: This typeface recreates their forms and atmosphere, including a “$” glyph that represents the background cathode formed by all digits layered together.
nixiePage.license: Released under CC BY-SA 4.0. You are free to use and adapt it with attribution.

akitraPage.storyP1: Older Taiwan Railways passenger and freight cars were painted with type codes, car numbers, tare weights, and load capacities. Lettered stroke by stroke, they carry the distinctive force of a handmade era.
akitraPage.storyP2: This typeface redraws that lettering and includes 284 frequently used glyphs, preserving visual memories that are gradually disappearing from the railway.
akitraPage.inUseDescription: Using Zhuyin symbols for rolling-stock type marks is a distinctive Taiwan Railways tradition.
akitraPage.license: Released under the SIL Open Font License 1.1 for free use in personal and commercial projects.

huninnPage.hero: A free, open-source Taiwanese rounded typeface—soft and lovable like tapioca pearls.
samples: The horizon is wide and the sky outside is blue
huninnPage.storyP1: jf open Huninn is an open-source typeface project initiated by justfont. Based on Kosugi Maru and Varela Round, it was refined for Taiwanese character standards and everyday use.
huninnPage.storyP2: I was fortunate to contribute while at justfont. Opening type-design work to the whole community was a truly valuable experience.
huninnPage.testerDescription: Type anything you want to test; the complete font will load here automatically.
huninnPage.license: Huninn is maintained by justfont and released under the SIL Open Font License 1.1.
huninnPage.loadError: The complete font could not be loaded; only available glyphs are shown.
huninnPage.defaultText: Like a pearl—round, soft, and springy.

tgJpgPage.hero: Type .jpg in a chat and let the bot reply with a meme. Supports .jpg, .png, and .gif.
tgJpgPage.ideaText: No need to leave the conversation to search for images. tg.jpg connects to Google, DuckDuckGo, and Bing, plus optional Serper and SerpAPI; with a Serper API key, it takes top priority.
steps: Listens for messages ending in .jpg, .png, or .gif.
Runs health checks on startup and enables only currently available engines.
Searches across sources; the first result Telegram can send wins immediately.
tgJpgPage.chatAlt: tg.jpg replying with a mic drop GIF in Telegram
tgJpgPage.rustText: Rewritten in Rust, with Teloxide for Telegram and Reqwest for search results. Each source is health-checked on startup; failed sources are disabled and the rest are tried in priority order. Includes Docker and Fly.io deployment configuration.

tokyonoPage.title: Tokyono Sora
tokyonoPage.hero: A free and open CSS theme for Plurk. Rework the timeline, glass panels, and responsive layout—just paste in the CSS.
tokyonoPage.designText: Tokyono Sora leaves Plurk’s core interactions intact and redraws its appearance: information hierarchy, color, spacing, and the relationship between the background and interface. It is pure CSS; paste it into Plurk’s custom theme style field to apply it.

features.title: Reworking the timeline
features.description: Line height, display names, avatar placement, color, and post width are all adjusted for easier scanning.

features.title: Blending background and UI
features.description: Translucent panels with live blur make an uploaded background genuinely part of the interface.

features.title: Readable in every orientation
features.description: Supports portrait screens and browser zoom without letting the layout fall apart.

features.title: Nixie-tube karma
features.description: Karma is displayed in a custom Nixie numeral typeface; custom.css can switch it to a world-line divergence style.

tokyonoPage.profileAlt: Glass profile panel with world-line-divergence karma styling
tokyonoPage.karmaAlt: Close-up of karma rendered in a Nixie numeral typeface
tokyonoPage.profileCaption: The profile panel and Nixie karma. Add one more custom.css block to switch to world-line-divergence styling.
tokyonoPage.rwdAlt: Responsive layout on a portrait screen
tokyonoPage.rwdCaption: Portrait-screen layout. Post width and component placement adapt to the window.
tokyonoPage.installText: Paste the theme CSS into Plurk’s Custom Theme → Custom Theme Style. Live blur uses the GPU; machines without hardware acceleration can precompute a background with the static blur generator. Extensions for dark mode and the top bar are included too.

kumikoPage.hero: Open a browser and add missing glyphs to open-source Chinese fonts on GitHub.
kumikoPage.imageAlt: Kumiko glyph and component editing interface
kumikoPage.idea: Lower the barrier to glyph completion to a URL
kumikoPage.ideaText: Open-source CJK fonts can be missing thousands of glyphs, but people who want to help may not want to assemble a full font toolchain. Kumiko loads a UFO project from GitHub, edits glyphs in the browser, and sends changes back as a pull request. No installation and no backend to run.
kumikoPage.workflow: From font source to pull request
kumikoPage.idsText: It includes an IDS (Ideographic Description Sequence) decomposition engine. To add 編, look up ⿰糹扁 and combine the existing 糹 and 扁 components. Completing a glyph becomes component adjustment rather than drawing a whole character from scratch.
kumikoPage.architecture: Architecture shaped by the user experience
features: Runs locally on the web. A browser is all you need.
Supports .ufo, .designspace, .glyphs, .glyphspackage, and binary fonts, or loads UFO directly from GitHub.
Runs fontTools through Pyodide, so no local Python environment is needed.
Edits are stored in browser IndexedDB and survive being offline or closing the tab.

splitFlapPage.hero: A React component that recreates station and airport split-flap displays. Pure CSS transform animations keep hundreds of flaps turning smoothly at once.
splitFlapPage.ideaText: Keikyu Kawasaki’s split-flap departure board retired in 2022, but the sound and rhythm of its mechanical motion remain captivating. react-split-flap recreates that feeling with CSS transforms: SplitFlap turns a string one character at a time, while LongFlap can turn an entire ReactNode—an image, icon, or a full layout—as one panel.
splitFlapPage.playgroundDescription: Type a few characters and watch each flap turn toward its target character.
splitFlapPage.inputLabel: Text shown on the split-flap display
splitFlapPage.clockText: A live clock that turns once per second. When the tab is in the background, its animation clock falls back to a coarse timer; it still shows the correct time when the tab returns to the foreground.

engineering.title: Shared animation clock
engineering.description: Every flap subscribes to one requestAnimationFrame loop. Turns that expire in the same frame are merged into a single React commit, so hundreds of simultaneous flaps render only once.

engineering.title: No DOM reconstruction
engineering.description: Flaps remain mounted without changing keys, alternating between two CSS animation names to start directly from the first frame. There is no per-flap Web Animations API read/write cycle.

engineering.title: Minimal compositing layers
engineering.description: Only the moving top and bottom halves are promoted to GPU layers. In a scene of about 400 flaps, this cuts compositing layers by about 70% and GPU time by about 27%.

splitFlapPage.boardDestinations: For Haneda Airport, Shinagawa, Shimbashi & Asakusa
splitFlapPage.boardCaption: A tribute to Keikyu Kawasaki Station’s pata-pata departure board, retired in 2022

hero.lead: The familiar composing experience and candidate window, now with Apple Silicon support.

features.title: The familiar way, made easier
features.description: Careful refinements preserve the familiar feel while improving security and performance.

items.title: Adaptive candidates
items.description: Candidate order combines the language model with your selection history. Frequently chosen characters rise to the top.

items.title: Add phrases
items.description: Hold Shift and press ← to expand the selection one character at a time, then press Enter to add it to your user dictionary. Ctrl+3 adds the three characters before the cursor directly (Ctrl+1–9 sets the length).

items.title: Symbol table
items.description: Control+Command+. opens the symbol table. When the composition area is empty, Ctrl+0 also shows punctuation candidates.

items.title: Traditional-to-Simplified Chinese
items.description: Control+Command+G toggles the output filter: type Bopomofo as usual and commit Simplified Chinese. Command+Shift+Space toggles full-width Latin characters.

items.title: Tab segmentation
items.description: Move the cursor to where you want a break while composing and press Tab to force segmentation—the sentence is then recomposed around that boundary.

items.title: The old friends are still here
items.description: The default is smart Bopomofo composition, while the traditional Bopomofo, Cangjie, and Simple modules from the OpenVanilla era remain available.

history.title: What is ChiaKey?
history.description: ChiaKey is a free, open-source revival of Yahoo! KeyKey, made to repair and improve it while recreating a lexicon with a familiar feel.
history.body: After ending the project, Yahoo! released its code as open source. ChiaKey starts from that codebase: replacing obsolete APIs, repairing missing pieces, and carefully improving functionality and performance so it can once again build, install, and type on modern macOS—and continue to be maintained.

timeline.title: Yahoo! KeyKey launches
timeline.description: A free, cross-platform intelligent input method.

timeline.title: Project closes; source code opens
timeline.description: Released under a BSD-style license on GitHub (YahooArchive/KeyKey). The final installer has been continuously adapted by the community ever since.

timeline.title: Apple announces the end of x86 support
timeline.description: The final-generation installer will no longer install on macOS 26 and later.

timeline.title: ChiaKey
timeline.description: Repairs the app and organizes compatible lexicons for Apple Silicon and modern macOS.

principles.title: Keep the familiar feel
principles.description: The first goal is to retain Yahoo! KeyKey’s composition, candidate window, and language model—the same way of typing.

principles.title: Modernize carefully
principles.description: Improve security and algorithms with modern APIs and compile for Apple Silicon.

principles.title: A conservative pace
principles.description: ChiaKey is not trying to compete on features with modern input methods. Its focus is stability.

lexicon.title: Layered lexicons that understand you
lexicon.description: A lexicon is more than one list. To restore the familiar feel, ChiaKey combines multiple sources and selects vocabulary suited to everyday Taiwanese usage; after installation on your Mac, it adds two layers that are yours.

dataLayers.title: Compatibility base
dataLayers.description: Maintains compatibility with the ChiaKey runtime, existing schemas, and module tables.

dataLayers.title: External lexicons
dataLayers.description: Mature Traditional Chinese vocabulary and readings from input methods such as Chewing and RIME.

dataLayers.title: Project lexicon
dataLayers.description: Our maintained lexicon: fixes known gaps, specifies readings, tunes candidate order, and adds Taiwanese usage, synthetic corpora, and unigram/bigram supplements extracted from trending words.

dataLayers.title: Correction layer
dataLayers.description: Small reviewed rules keep default zh-TW output aligned with common Taiwanese usage and cap fragment weights to prevent incorrect segmentation caused by character stealing.

localLayers.title: User dictionary
localLayers.description: Custom phrases added with Shift selection or Ctrl+[number] stay on your machine and can be imported or exported.

localLayers.title: Learning cache
localLayers.description: Your candidate selection history and word frequencies.

update.title: Update lexicons without reinstalling
update.before: The four data-layer lexicons are independently updated after
update.after: builds ChiaKey-Lexicon. The input method automatically checks and validates the newest lexicon before enabling it. Your custom phrases and selection history remain in the local database.

addPhrase.title: Add phrases
addPhrase.ariaLabel: Hold Shift and press the left arrow three times to select the phrase, then press Enter to add it to the user dictionary

conversion.title: Traditional-to-Simplified Chinese
conversion.ariaLabel: Press Control, Command, and G to enable Traditional-to-Simplified conversion; committed text becomes Simplified Chinese
conversion.note: Type and select candidates as usual; only committed output changes (Traditional → Simplified)

engines.title: Input modules
engines.ariaLabel: Typing demonstrations for smart Bopomofo, traditional Bopomofo, Cangjie, and Simple input modules

learning.title: Adaptive candidates
learning.ariaLabel: After selecting a candidate, it moves to the first position next time

segmentation.title: Tab segmentation
segmentation.ariaLabel: Move the cursor between words and press Tab to resegment the composition
segmentation.note: Force a boundary at the cursor and recompose the sentence

symbols.title: Symbol table
symbols.ariaLabel: Press Control, Command, and period to open the symbol table

download.title: Install
download.description: Download and install the .pkg, then add ChiaKey in System Settings → Keyboard → Input Sources. Apple Silicon is supported.
download.disclaimer: ChiaKey is not an official Yahoo product. Its source is released under a BSD-style license; the Yahoo! name and contributor names may not endorse derivative products without written permission.
audiences: You miss the feel of Yahoo! KeyKey
You want to preserve or study the KeyKey/OpenVanilla engine
You want a stable Bopomofo input method that does not change often
